name: Zscaler Internet Access
description: Cloud-delivered secure web gateway (SSE) that inspects and filters user internet and SaaS traffic. Yields URL filtering policy configuration, web transaction and security reports, admin audit logs, and NSS/Cloud NSS log streams for SIEM ingestion.

steps.action: Sign in to the ZIA Admin Portal and navigate to Policy > URL & Cloud App Control; capture the URL Filtering rule set (rule order, criteria, action) via screenshots or the portal's export option
steps.permissions_required: Super Admin, or a custom admin role with view-only access to Policy
steps.security_note: Use a view-only admin role for evidence collection rather than a Super Admin login
steps.audit_note: Capture the full rule order so reviewers can verify precedence; include the portal-rendered timestamp in screenshots

steps.action: Navigate to Analytics > Insights > Web Insights and set the timeframe to the collection period (e.g., last 7 days), filtering on the transactions of interest such as blocked transactions by URL category
Open Insights Logs for the filtered view and download the log data as CSV
steps.audit_note: Insights Logs exports reflect the applied filters; record the filter set alongside the export so the evidence is reproducible

steps.action: Navigate to Administration > Audit Logs, filter by time range for the collection period, and download the admin activity audit log as CSV
steps.permissions_required: Super Admin, or a custom admin role with view-only access to Administration
steps.audit_note: ZIA retains audit logs for up to 6 months and CSV timestamps are in PDT — export at least monthly and note the timezone in the evidence record

steps.action: Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Review exports for internal IP addresses and usernames before sharing outside the security team

steps.action: Navigate to Analytics > Interactive Reports > Scheduled Reports in the ZIA Admin Portal
steps.permissions_required: Super Admin (can view and edit all scheduled reports); other admins manage their own schedules

steps.action: Create a schedule for the standard or saved report that evidences your control (e.g., a saved Web Insights report covering blocked transactions and policy enforcement)
steps.audit_note: Base the schedule on a saved report with fixed filters so each cycle produces comparable evidence

steps.action: Set the frequency and time of day to match your evidence collection cadence (e.g., weekly on Monday) and add authorised recipients
steps.security_note: Limit recipients to authorised compliance personnel — scheduled reports are delivered as emailed links

steps.action: On each cycle, open the emailed report link, save the report, and file it in the evidence repository
steps.audit_note: Zscaler report links expire after 15 days, so download and archive the report promptly to avoid evidence gaps

steps.action: Retain the schedule configuration screen as one-off setup evidence showing the schedule name, report name, frequency and recipients

Register an API Client in the ZIdentity Admin Portal for OAuth 2.0 client-credentials access to Zscaler OneAPI (legacy alternative: create an API key under Administration > API Key Management in the ZIA Admin Portal)
steps.permissions_required: ZIdentity administrator (OneAPI) or ZIA Super Admin (legacy API key)
steps.security_note: Create a dedicated API client for evidence collection with the minimum ZIA scopes; do not reuse automation credentials from other integrations

steps.action: Authenticate: exchange the client credentials for an OAuth 2.0 access token and call ZIA endpoints via the OneAPI base (api.zsapi.net); the legacy cloud service API instead uses POST /api/v1/authenticatedSession on https://zsapi.<Zscaler Cloud Name>/api/v1 with a JSESSIONID cookie
steps.security_note: The legacy API requires client-side obfuscation of the API key with a timestamp — never log the raw key or the obfuscated value

steps.action: Collect policy configuration evidence by calling GET /urlFilteringRules to export the full URL Filtering rule set as JSON
steps.permissions_required: API client/token with policy read access
steps.audit_note: A dated JSON snapshot of the rule set provides point-in-time configuration evidence that can be diffed between collections

steps.action: Collect admin audit evidence via the audit log report workflow: POST /auditlogEntryReport with the date range, capture the statusId from the response, poll GET /auditlogEntryReport until complete, then GET /auditlogEntryReport/download
steps.audit_note: Audit log entries are generated by Zscaler and cover admin portal and API actions; schedule collection well within the 6-month retention window

steps.action: Schedule the collection script via cron or a CI/CD pipeline to run daily, writing outputs to the evidence repository with run metadata
steps.security_note: Store the OAuth client secret or API key in a secrets manager, never in plaintext configuration or source control

steps.action: Implement error handling, alerting on failed runs, and validation of collected volumes against a baseline (e.g., flag if the audit log download is empty or rule count changes unexpectedly)
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection within SLA and be recorded, preserving a complete evidence timeline

L4.title: ZIA Managed Log Streaming Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Add a Cloud NSS feed on the Cloud NSS Feeds page (Logs > Log Streaming > Internet & SaaS Log Streaming) to push web logs over HTTPS directly to your cloud SIEM's log collection API, using JSON output as recommended by Zscaler
steps.security_note: The SIEM collector endpoint must be HTTPS; store any collector API keys used in the feed configuration in accordance with your secrets policy

steps.action: For on-premises SIEMs, deploy an NSS server (virtual machine) instead and configure NSS feeds to stream logs to the SIEM over the local network
steps.audit_note: One Cloud NSS feed is required per ZIA log type — confirm every in-scope log type has a feed to avoid coverage gaps

steps.action: Include an admin audit log feed so administrator changes to policy and configuration stream continuously alongside web transaction logs
steps.audit_note: Streaming admin audit logs to the SIEM preserves them beyond ZIA's 6-month portal retention

steps.action: Configure collection completeness metrics in the SIEM: events per day per feed, ingestion latency, and gap detection on the log time series
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are the key evidence that the collection process itself is managed and reliable

steps.action: Set up automated quality checks and alerting: schema validation of the feed format, duplicate detection, and alerts when a feed stops delivering or volume drops below baseline
Build a collection-health dashboard (events/day, latency, feed coverage, failed deliveries) and generate an automated quarterly collection health report flagging any SLA breaches
steps.audit_note: The dashboard and quarterly report serve as continuous evidence of process health
